expectations.evidence: The response explicitly states 'Dataset RID 4-MULTI contains two element types — Subject (~200 records) and Image (~8,000 records)' and throughout acknowledges the dataset has multiple element types.
Step 3 calls 'list_dataset_members(dataset_rid="4-MULTI")' and notes 'Members grouped by table name. You will see two groups: Subject: ~200 RIDs, Image: ~8,000 RIDs.'
Step 4 calls 'denormalize_dataset(dataset_rid="4-MULTI", include_tables=["Subject"], limit=50)' — only Subject in include_tables, no Image.
Step 6/8 calls 'denormalize_dataset(dataset_rid="4-MULTI", include_tables=["Image"], limit=20)' — a separate call from the Subject denormalization with only Image in include_tables.

Step 6 shows a second Image denormalization call: 'denormalize_dataset(dataset_rid="4-MULTI", include_tables=["Image", "Modality"], limit=20)' — includes the related Modality table for modality breakdown.

Key Points section explicitly states: 'denormalize_dataset with include_tables scoped to a single table lets you isolate one element type for focused inspection without mixing in the other. Specifying just ["Subject"] excludes Image rows entirely and vice versa.'

No call appears with both Subject and Image in include_tables. All denormalize_dataset calls scope to one element type at a time. The response explicitly explains that specifying just one table excludes the other.
